title: Greedy coin change on a canonical denomination system

informal_statement.text:
Given a sorted-ascending slice of positive integer coin denominations that forms a canonical system (greedy is optimal), and a non-negative target amount, return a vector `counts` of the same length as the denominations such that `sum(counts[i] * denoms[i]) == amount` and `sum(counts)` is the minimum possible over all such vectors.

[Grid variant 002 — paired within-family control: distinct authoring emphasis on summary/scaffold hygiene, auxiliary lemmas, and scope discipline so obligations cannot smuggle hidden parameters. Algorithmic contract and oracle checks remain identical to variant 001.]
preconditions: every denomination is a positive integer
the denomination system is canonical (greedy yields the optimum)
required_properties: counts has the same length as denoms
sum over i of counts[i] * denoms[i] equals amount
sum of counts is minimum over all valid decompositions
edge_cases: amount exactly equal to a denomination
amount smaller than the second-smallest denomination
very large amount requiring many units of the penny
